For the AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson package, I'm running into a constraint with Central Package Management — I can't define two versions of the same package in Directory.Packages.props even though they're conditional on different target frameworks. for .NET 6 and 8.0.11 is for .NET 8 — these are framework-specific versions, so I can't just use the newer one for both. I need to set up conditional PackageVersion entries in Directory.Packages.props that target each framework separately.

Now I'm thinking about the user's instruction to use the highest version for conflicts. The framework-conditional packages aren't really conflicts since they're intentionally different versions for different targets — using 8.0.11 for net6.0 would break the build. But for actual conflicts like System.Text.Json where the same package has multiple versions listed, I should consolidate those to the highest version.

For the MSBuild properties, I need to inline the version values since CPM will centralize everything. My plan is to create a Directory.Packages.props file at the root with all the resolved package versions, then remove the old property definitions from the individual project files.
